# katapult_object_storage_account (Resource)

Manages the object storage account for an organization in a given region.

A Katapult organization has at most one object storage account per region. This resource creates the account (if it does not already exist) and waits for it to reach the `provisioned` state. All `katapult_object_storage_bucket` and `katapult_object_storage_access_key` resources must reference this resource via `object_storage_account_id`, which ties their lifecycle to the account and gives Terraform a way to clean the account up when no longer needed.

~> **Only declare one of these per (organization, region).** If your organization already has object storage enabled via the Katapult dashboard, declare this resource anyway and import the existing account — otherwise Terraform cannot clean it up on destroy, and your organization will continue to be billed.

~> **Only one of these resources may exist per (organization, region).**
The Katapult API enforces this — a second `katapult_object_storage_account`
block for the same region will fail to create. If your organization already
has object storage enabled via the Katapult dashboard, declare this resource
anyway and **import the existing account** rather than skipping it.
Otherwise Terraform cannot clean the account up on destroy, and your
organization will continue to be billed.

The account is the entrypoint for everything else in this provider's object
storage surface:

* [`katapult_object_storage_bucket`](./object_storage_bucket.md) resources
  attach to an account via `object_storage_account_id`.
* [`katapult_object_storage_access_key`](./object_storage_access_key.md)
  resources attach to an account via `object_storage_account_id`.
* Destroying this resource is **blocked** while any buckets or access keys
  still exist in the region — managed by Terraform or not. The error message
  lists what is still present so you can clean up before retrying.
* On successful destroy, the account is purged from Katapult's trash
  immediately (otherwise it would linger and continue to be billed for
  up to 48 hours). Set the provider's `skip_trash_object_purge` option to
  `true` if you want to keep the account in the trash for manual recovery.

## Regions

Object storage is currently available in a single region, `uk-lon-1` (London,
UK). `region` defaults to this value and can be omitted. The resource is
multi-region-ready: as additional regions become available you can declare a
separate `katapult_object_storage_account` per region, and buckets and keys
opt in by referencing the right account.

## Minimal Usage

Copy this block verbatim if you don't already have object storage enabled —
it is the only sensible shape for a single-region setup.

```terraform
resource "katapult_object_storage_account" "main" {
  region = "uk-lon-1"
}
```

## Adopting an Existing Account

There are two ways to bring a pre-existing object storage account under
Terraform management:

**Recommended — `terraform import`:**

```shell
terraform import katapult_object_storage_account.main uk-lon-1
```

Then add the corresponding `resource` block to your configuration. After the
next `terraform plan`, the resource should report no changes.

**Alternative — `adopt_existing = true`:** for migration workflows where
running `terraform import` is impractical, set:

```terraform
resource "katapult_object_storage_account" "main" {
  region         = "uk-lon-1"
  adopt_existing = true
}
```

The first `terraform apply` will detect the existing account and silently
take ownership of it instead of erroring. **Use with care** — this flag
disables the safety check that prevents accidentally claiming an account
that belongs to a different setup. Prefer `terraform import` for anything
production-bound.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Only one of these may exist per (organization, region).
#
# If your organization already has object storage enabled via the Katapult
# dashboard, import the existing account rather than creating a new one:
#
#   terraform import katapult_object_storage_account.main uk-lon-1
resource "katapult_object_storage_account" "main" {
  region = "uk-lon-1"
}

output "account_provisioning_state" {
  value = katapult_object_storage_account.main.provisioning_state
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `adopt_existing` (Boolean) Adopt an existing object storage account for this region if one already exists, instead of erroring with import instructions. Defaults to `false`. Changing this forces replacement.
- `region` (String) Region permalink, e.g. `uk-lon-1`. Defaults to `uk-lon-1`. Changing this forces replacement.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Account identifier — the region permalink. Reference this from `katapult_object_storage_bucket` and `katapult_object_storage_access_key` via `object_storage_account_id`.
- `provisioning_state` (String) Current provisioning state of the account: `provisioning`, `provisioned`, or `failed`.

## Import

An account is imported using its region permalink. The organization is
inferred from the provider configuration.

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import katapult_object_storage_account.example uk-lon-1
```

## Related Resources

* [`katapult_object_storage_bucket`](./object_storage_bucket.md) — buckets
  inside this account.
* [`katapult_object_storage_access_key`](./object_storage_access_key.md) —
  credentials for clients accessing buckets in this account.
* [`katapult_object_storage_account`](../data-sources/object_storage_account.md)
  data source — read details for an account managed elsewhere.
